'''Veshok''' [[Tree/Legends|trees]] were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of slow-growing evergreen trees that were [[Homeworld/Legends|indigenous]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. Veshok trees grew in abundance on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world, and sprawling veshok [[Forest/Legends|forests]] covered most of Mandalore's northern hemisphere. Veshok trees grew tall, and produced hard, sturdy [[Veshok wood|wood]] that Mandalorians often put to use in a variety of projects, ranging from crafted household furniture, to simple [[Youngling/Legends|children's]] toys.

==Description==
Veshok [[Tree/Legends|trees]] were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of slow-growing,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> large, evergreen trees<ref name="Dictionary" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> Veshok trees grew in abundance on Mandalore, and sprawling veshok forests covered most of Mandalore's northern hemisphere, stretching nearly as far as the planet's polar [[Water/Legends|ice]] cap.<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref> Veshok trees produced hard, sturdy wood,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> with a grain similar to that of [[oak tree]]s.<ref name="Dictionary" />

==History==
Through the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], numerous [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] elected to make their homes in and around clusters of veshok trees. Such was the prevalence of veshok trees on Mandalore, that it was not uncommon for Mandalorians to put [[veshok wood]] to use in the construction of homes,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> furniture,<ref name="O66" /> outdoor fencing and gateposts, and even toys for [[Youngling/Legends|children]].<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>

During the time of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], the [[Veshok Terrace Apartments]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] shared their name with the tough Mandalorian trees.<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref> Several millennia later, [[Veshok Squad]]―a unit of [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[Special Operations Brigade/Legends|Special Operations Brigade]]―was likewise named after the sturdy veshok tree.<ref name="O66" />

[[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]]—the home of the Mandalorian [[Clan Skirata|Skirata clan]] and a refuge for [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] deserters from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] Grand [[Army/Legends|Army]] both during and after the [[Declaration of a New Order|end]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]—was located near a large number of veshok trees. The homestead's inhabitants made use of the tree's strong wood in various aspects of the Mandalorian bastion, including a portion of the large ''[[vheh'yaim]]''{{'s}} furnishings, and in the creation of Kyrimorut's outdoor [[Roba/Legends|roba]] pin. The Skirata clan also used the surrounding veshok trees for wood to [[Fire/Legends|burn]] in Kyrimorut's [[Fireplace|hearths]].<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

When the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attacked]] Mandalore during their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], a large percentage of Mandalore's veshok trees were destroyed in the resulting battle. Much of the tree-based communities that made their home in the veshok forests around Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], were completely wiped out alongside the veshok trees destroyed by the Yuuzhan Vong. However, by the year [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Mandalore's veshok forests were beginning to return, growing back toward their former prominence.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

==Behind the scenes==
Veshok trees were first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novel, [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice|''Sacrifice'']], written by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]] and published [[May 29]], [[2007]].<ref name="Sacrifice" /> In the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] dictionary compiled by Traviss, she noted that though veshok trees were intended to be large evergreens, they were possessed of a wood grain more similar to that of oak trees.<ref name="Dictionary" />
